Loess hills bluffs, Council Bluffs, Iowa, north of I-80
Watershed dam and othe conservation measures in the loess hills region of western Iowa.

gallery.desc: A good example of the thin ridge that forms the "spine" of the Loess Hills.
A view of the thin ridges that form the "spine" the Loess Hills.
Turin at the base of the Loess Hills

gallery.cap: Loess Plains (south of Maxwell, Nebraska, USA) 2
gallery.desc: The hills in the distance are part of the "Loess Plains" of Nebraska. "Plains" implies a planar or level landscape. The irregular topography is the result of erosion and dissection. "Loess" refers to wind-blown silt deposits. The silt was usually derived from glacial sediments in ancient rivers that drained melting summertime glaciers during the Pleistocene Ice Age. Some loess deposits consist of
